titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5952

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=792 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.870229+00 | translation moa=793 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.879556+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus has litteras uisuris vel audituris . Bertramus filius Ade filii Cospatricii de Parua Ristona Salutem . Nouerit vniuersitas vestra 
me intuitu caritatis et pro salute anime mee . et Margarete vxoris mee . et pro salute patris mei et matris mee . et Roberti filii mei . et
predeceossorum et successorum meorum . dedisse . concessisse . et presenti hac carta confirmasse deo et sancte Marie . et promocioni Ecclesie de Codlginham
in puram et perpetuam elemosinam . nouem acras terre in Territorio parue Ristone Scilicet . quatuor acras in Blackechet’ . iuxta
Farnebed . et quinque acras in Goselauflat . tenendas et habendas de me et heredibus meis in puram et perpetuam elemosina inperpetuum . cum communi pastura
et omnibus libertatibus et aysiamentis ad tantam terram infra uillam et extra pertinentibus . Quare uolo et concedo ut predicta Ecclesia . prefatas nouem acras
terre habeat et inperpetuum possideat libere . quiete . et absolute ab omnibus operibus seruiciis secualribus auxilius exactionibus et demandis . Salua multura
et decima domui de Coldginham . Ego uero et heredes mei prefatas nouem acras terre sepedicte ecclesie contra omnes homines et feminas warantizabimus
Et ut hec mea donacion rata et onconcussa semper permaneat  huic scripto sigillum meum apposui . Hiis Testibus . Domino . A . Priore tunc temporis de Coldingham
Domino Willelmo de Mordingtona . Domino Alano de Suintona . Alano filio eius . Ada de Prenderguest . Willelmo de Scremerstona . Willelmo vicario de Aldecambus
Iohanne filio helie . Ada tunc marescallo . Roberto filio Gregorii Senescaldi . et multis aliis .

p.cl: To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear these letters, Bertram, son of Adam son of Cospatric of Little Reston, sends Greeting.

moved by charity and for the salvation of my own soul, and of my wife Margaret's, and for the salvation of my father and my mother, and of Robert my son, and my predecessors and successors, to have given, granted, and to have made firm by this present charter, to God and to Saint Mary, and for the improvement of the church of Coldingham, in pure and perpetual alms, nine acres of land in the territory of Little Reston, that is, four acres in Blackechet' near Farnebed, and five acres in Goselauflat, to be held and had of me and my heirs in pure and everlasting alms for ever, with common pasture and all liberties and easements pertaining to so much land inside and outside the toun. Wherefore I will and grant that the aforesaid church should have and possess the aforementioned nine acres of land for ever, free, quit, and released from all works, secular services, aids, exactions and demands; saving multure and the teinds of the house of Coldingham. And I and my heirs shall warrant the aforementioned nine acres of land to the oft-mentioned church against all men and women.
And so that my donation should ever remain valid and undisputed, I have applied my seal to this writing.

With these witnesses: the lord A(nketin), then prior of Coldingham; the lord William of Mordington; the lord Alan of Swinton; Alan his son; Adam of Prenderguest; William of Scremerston; William, vicar of Oldcambus; John son of Elias; Adam, then marischal; Robert, son of Gregory the Steward; and many others.
